item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: No source found indicates the image that was used in this article has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, it is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the SCP, but I can't take it seriously with that picture lol

item.title: Re: Amnestic Doubt
item.content:encoded: The severity of amnestics is usually more of a headcanon. Some might treat Class B to be more severe than Class A, and vice versa. Ultimately, there is no authoritative guide on the severity of amnestics.

item.title: Amnestic Doubt
item.content:encoded: Why using Class A amnestic which only removes some hours of memory instead of Class B? To become a "local attraction" surely it passed at least a couple of days since its retrieval

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically, there is a lolFoundation canon. But lolFoundation in mainlisters are effectively dead; I guess most of the readership and authorship decide that office shenanigans make no sense when one works with really dangerous objects all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The day I can't find lolfoundation addenda on SCPs is the day I jsut don't come here anymore.

If that's the case, then I'm sorry to say that you should be prepared to stop browsing the site one day. Lolfoundation is no longer considered acceptable to site standards, outside of -J's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The day I can't find lolfoundation addenda on SCPs is the day I jsut don't come here anymore. They're the best part of almost every article that has them, many don't work without them, and several have been ruined by failed attempts to be serious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main question is that, since the truck uses as much diesel as a full-sized one and can store as much as one, but has a reduced physical size and therefore a reduced fuel inlet cross-section, how does the Foundation refuel it?

I could easily imagine an object like this being used under special circumstances in the Foundation's operations, specifically for transporting supermassive objects in cases where there is a limited amount of space to transport the object in. e.g. moving the neutron star marble out of a confined bunker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how the caption to the picture says the SCP is being transported to another Site, like the Foundation was like "Screw it, let's just transport it out in the open. Nobody would believe it anyway."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot, but it needs some revision. The note doesn't sound professional and I don't understand the point of storing the controller in a desk instead of a storage facility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I can't not downvote part of it being stored in a desk. Get rid of that and I'd grant a +2 swing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just had to say after reading this again that I still really like it. And after reading a lot more of the entries, I kinda see the lolFoundation more, but it still isn't that bad, imo.

The addendum could maybe be tweaked just a little so it gets the point across without sounding quite so berating of the educated scientists. Because it looks like a toy, people may reasonably forget or underestimate the level of inertia the thing can have if they got it up to speed during a test.

item.title: Re: Dump truck weight
item.content:encoded:
Truck driver here. It's a dumb nitpick, but that tractor trailer would need… on the order of thirteen or fourteen axles, napkin math.

I know it doesn't matter but I just wanted to note that.

item.title: Re: Dump truck weight
item.content:encoded: You're thinking about commercial dump trucks. Some of the larger, more specialized ones (of which SCP-115 is clearly visually based off of) can weigh up to 100 tons.

item.title: Dump truck weight
item.content:encoded:
I feel compelled to point out that at 90 tons empty, this SCP is most likely the heaviest dump truck to exist. Normal dump trucks (which this resembles in all manners save size) weigh on the order of 13-20 tons, and have a load-carrying capacity on the order of 40 tons.

90 tons is more like the weight of a 757, or a full-grown blue whale (the largest known animal).

item.title: thats exactly...
item.content:encoded: That's exactly what I thought ^~^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Accidents can happen, sure. But weren't reseachers from the Foundation supposed to be among the best on their fields of expertise? In fact, let's disregard that: One thing is being careless and another, very different thing is using a note to remind adults that they should abide to basic security protocols.

I like the article and we've got to account that this is from '09, but I don't think this would be how a super secret, transnational organization would operate at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In what universe are Foundation employees never careless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I can understand that. I still like this one, though. =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lolfoundation is really when researchers act like little children. The last note implies that researchers were careless with this thing, which shouldn't be the case with Foundation Employees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the lolFoundation problems people have. I know I'm still new, but this is humorous, yes. At the same time, it's funny because it's quirky, not because it's downright hilarious like most of the -Js. I can totally see something like this existing in the Scipiverse. And really, a lot of items like that will exist that have slightly lolwut discoveries. The tothbrush was likely discovered because someone dropped their toothbrush and the bristles cut through the floor and the thing stuck upright out of the ground or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I permitted to affix a wooden platform to the back of it, and ride it at high speeds no wait, that's doctor bright's job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are perfectly logical questions which will probably never be answered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I can ask, why is the name of the person who has the controller censored? Wouldn't the fact that it was in his desk mean people would have regular access to it? And wouldn't they need to know who he is so that they could access it? Otherwise it would just be locked In a normal storage locker. Right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only if you write it well.

That's the thing - only the good fiction lasts here, so it's highly probable the "And then everything goes wrong!" will be the result - it's more interesting when using SCP-914 that a wooden puzzle containing the researchers keys gets made then using SCP-914 to make a jet pack or a rave party drug ball…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In either case: It still needs really secure containment. The SCP foundation may or may not want to make use of it. But a highly weaponisable SCP is right up the alley of several GoIs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We generally don't weaponize SCPs. The things tend to have unpredictable properties (ha, no shit, Photo!), and a combat situation is the last place you want to discover that your "tractable SCP" weapon goes ballistic and omnicidal at the sight of blood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this thing is a highly potent weaponisable SCP, it should be well contained, and ready for use by a mobile task force if needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It can hold a lot of matter in a tiny volume for carrying, no reason it can't do that for fuel storage too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. I realize not everything needs to be edited to "new standards", but LOLfoundation needs to be burned until there is not an atom of it left outside the -J's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Otherwise I really like this article, but the lolfoundation and the desk drawer containment procedures unfortunately make me downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually there are a lot of picture like that around. Truckers and construction workers with a sense of humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good work on that post, you managed to make a ridiculous looking picture (that was probably created as a joke) into a well-written SCP.

I'm curious about the fuel, however. Considered the fact that the truck must have a relatively small area to confine it's fuel and yet still consumes as much as an actual truck, wouldn't that means it would be emptied in a very fast manner?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, most of the older articles were made with different standards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess this article is from back in the days when lolfoundation addenda were still tolerated?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This related?

item.title: Re: Another picture
item.content:encoded: Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture is so precious! XD

item.title: Another picture
item.content:encoded:
Incidentally, I have another picture of this.
no pictures in the forums, thanks -Bright
Please feel free to use it. I took it myself in Columbia, SC.
Hope you can use it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, the second addenda is the only one that irks me. I've tried to lift things that were too heavy for me, and surprisingly I did NOT break my back. (I have thrown it out and pulled muscles, though.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article, but I feel kind of dirty to have just upvoted something with two humorous addenda attached.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tonka tough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've a feeling a lot of bad things would happen to everybody if Kondraki were to obtain this.

item.title: Re: Wait, what?!
item.content:encoded: Ah, I missed that. Thanks for the catch.

item.title: Re: Wait, what?!
item.content:encoded:
SCP-115-2 is the controller, and that is what is stored in the drawer. SCP-115-1 is the dump truck itself, and that is in the Secure Vehicles Storage Facility.

No clue about the second part.

item.title: Wait, what?!
item.content:encoded:
I agree that this is an excellent article, but I do have a question.

How in the hell do they have the thing stored in a desk drawer? It's ninety tons!

There's also the question on how it doesn't manage to sink into the ground, being that it's got to have a density approaching that of a star's core. Maybe it doesn't really weigh that much, but rather has the inertia of that much mass.

Interesting bits of speculation. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this a lot. I might remove the humor quotes, but it's a solid entry overall, and easily nails the "what the hell?" feeling. It's also well written, and fits the style well. No flowery text, no emotional "we have no idea how this could be!" sections, just a toy truck that is, for all intents and purposes, a real one. The bit about needing and storing the same amount of fuel was great, too.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Agreed. The picture really makes the article here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like this one. It's not scary, but it's quirky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is an awesome picture
